meta.title: Strategic Finance Forum & Gala | Digital Flagship
meta.description: Asset resilience in a volatile economy — an executive forum where academia, banking, and institutional finance shape the next generation of capital allocation. Sofia, May 2026.

nav.facultyTagline: Faculty of Economics and Business Administration (FEBA)

hero.themeLine: Asset Resilience in a Volatile Economy
hero.title: Strategic Finance Forum & Gala
hero.subtitle: The digital flagship for an elite strategic dialogue — where capital, regulation, research, and innovation meet. Not a conventional conference: a Quadruple Helix convening for C-suite banking, policymakers, scholars, and corporate innovators redefining how institutions price uncertainty and build resilience.

about.title: Why This Forum Matters
about.body: Volatility is not merely a risk to mitigate — it is information to price. This forum treats uncertainty as an input to strategic choice: real options, dynamic risk frameworks, and adaptive capital allocation under stress. It is where theory meets implementation — from static equilibrium models toward institutions that thrive when regimes shift. Participants operate at the intersection of academic rigor, institutional finance, geopolitical strategy, and banking innovation — the caliber of discourse associated with premier executive summits and private capital fora.

themes.title: Strategic Themes
themes.subtitle: Conceptual anchors for dialogue — volatility as an asset class, resilience as a balance-sheet discipline, and finance for the green transition.

items.title: Volatility as Strategic Input
items.description: Pricing uncertainty rather than fearing it — real options logic, convexity, and hedging under radical unknowns.

items.title: Dynamic Risk & Resilience
items.description: From static VaR-like snapshots to adaptive frameworks that stress capital, liquidity, and institutional capability in parallel.

items.title: Green Transition Finance
items.description: Channeling institutional capital toward transition assets without diluting fiduciary rigor — policy, disclosure, and market design.

items.title: Where Theory Meets Implementation
items.description: Closing the gap between frontier research and trading rooms, treasury functions, and supervisory practice.

program.title: Executive Agenda
program.subtitle: A disciplined day built for decision-makers — strategic exchange, not spectacle.

speakers.title: Voices in Strategic Tension
speakers.subtitle: Intellectual actors in dialogue — editorial roles, not mere speaker slots.

list.name: Prof. Stanislav Gyoshev
list.role: Chair, Faculty of Economics and Business Administration
list.bio: Frames the institutional grammar of the forum — where academic authority meets executive expectation and long-horizon market design.

list.name: Elena Petrova
list.bio: Stress-tests consensus on growth and allocation — cross-border mandates, institutional discipline, and the politics of capital.

list.name: Daniel Marinov
list.bio: Grounds debate in macro transmission, liquidity cycles, and the early signals that separate noise from regime change.

media.title: Multimedia

ecosystem.title: Building Financial Innovation Ecosystems
ecosystem.subtitle: C4BG as strategic convener
ecosystem.body: C4BG operates as more than a host logo: it is an institutional partner and innovation ecosystem builder — bridging doctoral-level research with boardroom execution. The Strategic Finance Forum is one surface of that work: a venue where banking C-suites, regulators, scholars, and corporate innovators execute the Quadruple Helix in practice — converging capital, oversight, inquiry, and applied innovation.

partnerships.title: Strategic Quantitative Partnerships
partnerships.subtitle: Flagship research collaboration — elevate the site from a single date to an enduring platform.
partnerships.body: Corporate PhD pathways, R&D-as-a-service, and applied quantitative innovation with real-world data and academic rigor. Partners gain structured access to frontier methods — stochastic modeling, empirical asset pricing, and institutional design — aligned to live business and policy questions rather than abstract exercises.

future.title: Beyond 2026
future.subtitle: An enduring strategic platform — not a single evening in May.
future.body: The Strategic Finance Forum anchors a forward calendar: deepening corporate PhD and quantitative partnerships, preparing the Joint Academic and Finance Forum (JAAF) horizon, and serial convening that sustains Quadruple Helix dialogue across election cycles and market regimes.

items.label: Strategic Finance Forum
items.detail: Annual executive anchor — asset resilience, institutional design, gala-level convening.

items.label: JAAF 2027
items.detail: Academic–finance summit on the horizon — scholarly depth with market-facing relevance.

items.label: Corporate PhD & quant partnerships
items.detail: R&D-as-a-service and doctoral pathways tied to live institutional problems.

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.text: C4BG serves as an institutional bridge across academia, corporate leadership, policy experts, and media. The forum is designed to translate research-grade ideas into actionable partnerships and durable collaboration.
